metadata.description: SQL 语言: SQL is the standard query language for relational databases, divided into DDL (Data Definition Language), DML (Data Manipulation Language), and DCL (Data Control Language). Here are brief explanations of each component with a corresponding Python code example using the SQLAlchemy library: 1. **DDL (Data Definition Language)**: This part of SQL is used to define the structure of the database, such as creating tables. ```python from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker Base = declarative_base() class User(Base): __tablename__ = 'users' id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True) name = Column(String) engine = create_engine('sqlite:///example.db') Base.metadata.create_all(engine) # This creates the table ``` 2. **DML (Data Manipulation Language)**: This part of SQL is used to manipulate data in the database, such as inserting, updating, and deleting records. ```python Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine) session = Session() new_user = User(name='John Doe') session.add(new_user) # Insert a record session.commit() # Save changes user = session.query(User).filter_by(name='John Doe').first() user.name = 'Jane Doe' # Update a record session.commit() session.delete(user) # Delete a record session.commit() ``` 3. **DCL (Data Control Language)**: This part of SQL is used to control access permissions and other security-related aspects. ```python from sqlalchemy import func # Granting read-only permission to a user from sqlalchemy.orm import Session with Session(engine) as session: session.execute( "GRANT SELECT ON users TO 'readonly_user'" ) session.commit() ``` These examples provide a basic understanding of how DDL, DML, and DCL can be implemented in Python using SQLAlchemy.
